title: Assistant Nurse Manager
description:
Responsibilities:

Clinical Activities

The Assistant Nurse Manager ensures smooth daily clinical operations and continuity of patient care by:

· Overseeing daily ward workflow and patient care coordination

· Supporting complex clinical decision-making and escalation processes

· Conducting regular ward rounds to monitor care standards and patient safety

· Ensuring adherence to nursing protocols, care pathways, and clinical SOPs

· Supporting high-acuity or high-risk patient management (falls risk, pressure injuries, MDRO, tracheostomy care, wound management, etc.)

· Ensuring appropriate clinical handovers and continuity of care across shifts

· Providing on-site clinical support during emergencies and deteriorating patient situations

· Participate actively in clinical improvement projects.

Staff Supervision & Development

The Assistant Nurse Manager plays a key role in building a competent and professional nursing workforce by:

· Supervising daily nursing activities and task delegation

· Providing coaching, mentorship and real-time feedback to staff

· Supporting orientation and onboarding of new hires and locum staff

· Facilitating clinical competency assessments and skills validation

· Identifying staff training needs and coordinating continuous education activities

· Supporting performance appraisals, probation reviews and development planning

· Reinforcing professional conduct, workplace discipline and ethical practice

Quality & Patient Safety Initiatives

The Assistant Nurse Manager actively contributes to quality improvement and patient safety programmes by:

· Participating in quality improvement projects aligned with organisational KPIs

· Leading or supporting clinical audits (medication safety, falls prevention, infection control, documentation compliance)

· Monitoring incident trends and supporting corrective action planning

· Facilitating Root Cause Analysis (RCA) participation when required

· Promoting safety culture and reporting compliance among staff

· Implementing evidence-based practices to improve patient outcomes

· Tracking improvement outcomes and reporting progress to management

· Collaborate with nursing, allied health professionals, and support care teams to orchestrate quality improvement endeavours and continuous improvement projects

Resource & Manpower Coordination

The Assistant Nurse Managersupports effective manpower planning and resource utilisation by :

· Assisting with duty roster planning and overseeing shift allocation when required

· Ensuring safe staffing levels and skill mix across shifts

· Coordinating manpower coverage during sick leave, emergencies and peak workload periods

· Monitoring staff attendance, punctuality and overtime utilisation

· Ensure all leave applications are reviewed and approved in a timely manner.

· Practicing stewardship when supporting deployment of locum or agency staff when required

· Ensuring fair workload distribution across nursing teams

· Supporting equipment availability and ward supply readiness

Regulatory Compliance & Audits

The Assistant Nurse Manager ensures operational alignment with regulatory and governance standards by:

· Supporting compliance with Hospital to Home (HHOM), Ministry of Health (MOH) licensing conditions and SNB professional standards

· Ensuring staff adherence to organisational policies and SOPs

· Participating in internal and external audits and inspections

· Supporting documentation readiness for regulatory reviews

· Monitoring compliance with infection prevention and control standards

· Identifying gaps and implementing corrective actions

· Supporting accreditation and licensing preparation exercises

· Collect data required to meet audit and performance management targets in a timely manner.

Administrative Reporting & Documentation

The Assistant Nurse Manager ensures accurate operational documentation and reporting by:

· Supporting preparation of nursing operational reports and dashboards

· Monitoring documentation compliance in patient records and clinical systems

· Supporting incident reporting, audit submissions and regulatory documentation

· Maintaining staff training and competency records

· Supporting inventory tracking and equipment documentation

· Coordinating administrative communications between nursing, HR, operations and management teams

Systems and Administrative Support

· Assist in realignment of clinical, nursing and personal care staff within the continuum care system.

· Answer queries from internal and external parties in relation to clinical and nursing matters.

· Provide administrative support in clinical quality improvement projects and programmes.

· Comply with the organisation approved standards, procedures, protocols and policies.

· To adhere to the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) relating to clients and staff.

· Perform other related duties as assigned by Reporting Officer / Head of Nursing.

Requirements:

· Possess a Registered Nursing Certificate

· Registered with Singapore Nursing Board

· Minimally possess a Diploma in Nursing and Advanced Diploma in a speciality (preferably Medical, Geriatrics, Palliative

· Possess a professional demeanour and conduct themselves with integrity and respect towards all patients, colleagues and other parties

· Demonstrate patience and approachability while assisting patients and handling challenging situations effectively

· Demonstrate effective communication skills, both verbally and in writing, to interact with patients, colleagues and other parties

· Display adaptability and flexibility to work efficiently in diverse settings with changing demands and schedules

· Familiarity with regulatory guidelines and compliance standards.

· Possess good interpersonal and communication skills, with a positive work attitude and strong commitment towards service excellence

· Be a mature and objective lead, possess leadership qualities and positive interpersonal skills.

· Possess up-to-date Basic Cardia Life Support (BCLS) + Automated External Defibrillator (AED) Certificate.

· Must be able to work shift, occasional weekends and night shifts and public holidays monthly.

· Willing to go on rotational on-call roster weekly.

Work Location- 1800 Upper Changi Road North Singapore 507695

Please submit your application via the below link:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf1gwpmjTSjoXIkh2dbZmgR2Swm9UNAjOZ8lDidwR0jP0W3Gw/viewform?fbzx=8506684303558776261

postedCompany.description:
Your Health Comes First

Welcome to PanCare Medical Clinic. Our team is dedicated to providing patients with comprehensive services to benefit their health and well-being. With our streamlined registration system and responsive diagnosis procedure, we have more time to focus on helping patients find treatments to feel better, and connecting them with resources to help them stay that way.

Primary Care

Ranging from acute illnesses to chronic diseases, our team is dedicated to be your partner in providing well-rounded care for you and your family.

Health Screening

PanCare provides a wide range of health screening services personalized for each individual.

Vaccinations and Travel Medicine

We get you ready for your travel plans, caring for you so that you can enjoy yourself with a peace of mind.

Eye Screening

Eye screening services are available for all patients from young to old.

Skin and Hair

Eczema, acne, hair loss or other skin problems? PanCare will be able to help.


Minor Surgery

PanCare can also help you with your ingrown toe nails, viral warts, stitches for wounds or other minor surgeries.
postedCompany.name: PANCARE MEDICAL CLINIC PTE. LTD.
